records.ety: [AS. nēhst, nihst, nhst, superl. of neh nigh. See Nigh.]

defs: Nearest in place; having no similar object intervening.
quotes: Her princely guest Was next her side; in order sat the rest.
Fear followed me so hard, that I fled the next way.

defs: Nearest in time; as, the next day or hour.
Adjoining in a series; immediately preceding or following in order.
quotes: None could tell whose turn should be the next.

defs: Nearest in degree, quality, rank, right, or relation; as, the next heir was an infant.
quotes: The man is near of kin unto us, one of our next kinsmen.
notes: ☞ Next is usually followed by to before an object, but to is sometimes omitted. In such cases next in considered by many grammarians as a preposition.

defs: In the time, place, or order nearest or immediately succeeding; as, this man follows next.
